title: Quick flatbread
subtitle: Unleavened pan bread ready in 20 minutes
excerpt: A simple flatbread made with plain flour, olive oil, and water. No yeast, no resting time beyond a few minutes, cooked on a dry pan until charred in spots. Six flatbreads in under 20 minutes from bowl to table.
sourceNotes: Unleavened flatbreads are among the oldest forms of bread across every culture that had grain and fire. This version draws on the British-domestic tradition of a quick pan flatbread made with plain flour and oil, used as an alternative to pitta or tortilla. The technique is consistent across Middle Eastern lavash, Indian roti, and Turkish gozleme; this recipe takes the simplest elements.

recipe.freezeNotes: Stack cooked flatbreads with baking paper between each one. Freeze flat and reheat in a dry pan for 30 seconds a side from frozen.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles cleanly. Make the full batch of dough and cook in sequence.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dough keeps well-wrapped in the fridge for up to 24 hours. Bring to room temperature for 10 minutes before rolling.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: dry frying pan, medium-high heat; the pan is ready when a drop of water evaporates on contact

glossaryTerms.definition: The dough for a quick flatbread should feel smooth, soft, and only slightly tacky. If it sticks to the work surface when you pick it up cleanly, add more flour, a teaspoon at a time. A short 5-minute rest makes the dough easier to roll thin.

content.text: This is bread for the kitchen drawer when there is no bread in the house. The
comes together in 5 minutes and rests for another 5 while the pan heats. The flatbreads char nicely in spots and stay pliable once off the heat.

Combine 300 g flour and 4 g salt in a bowl. Add 30 ml olive oil and 145 ml lukewarm water. Mix to a shaggy dough, then turn onto a work surface and knead for 3 minutes until smooth. Divide into 6 pieces, roll each into a ball, cover with a damp cloth and rest for 5 minutes. The rest makes the dough noticeably easier to roll.

Heat a dry frying pan over medium-high heat. While it heats, roll one dough ball on a lightly floured surface into a thin circle or oval, about 20 to 22 cm across and 2 mm thick. Place in the dry pan. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes until bubbles form and the underside has golden-brown spots. Flip and cook for another 1 minute. Transfer to a plate and cover with a cloth to keep pliable. Roll and cook the remaining flatbreads in sequence.

items.problem: Flatbread comes out stiff and crisp rather than pliable
items.solution: Cover immediately after cooking and keep in the cloth. The trapped steam keeps the bread soft. Overcooked flatbreads become cracker-like; reduce the heat or shorten the cook time.

items.problem: Dough springs back every time you try to roll it
items.solution: The gluten is tight. Leave the dough balls covered for another 5 minutes. If they still spring back, the rest was too short or the dough was kneaded too long.
